zh.Walton on the Hill, Surrey, England: 英國素瑞沃爾頓 奧 馮 希爾
zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Bold Leigh of Walton是Robert Legh的女兒，於1448年出生在英國素瑞沃爾頓 奧 馮 希爾。她於1471年與Henry Leigh of Walton在英國英格蘭素瑞結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1492年在英國素瑞沃爾頓享年44歲。

zh-hans.Walton on the Hill, Surrey, England: 英国素瑞沃爾頓 奧 馮 希爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Bold Leigh of Walton是Robert Legh的女儿，于1448年出生在英国素瑞沃爾頓 奧 馮 希爾。她于1471年与Henry Leigh of Walton在英国英格兰素瑞结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1492年在英国素瑞沃爾頓享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Staffordshire): from Middle English bold ‘dwelling-house’ (Old English bold the West Midland and northwestern form of Old English bōthl, bōtl), especially in the sense ‘castle or manor house’ for someone who lived or worked at the main house, or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word, for example Bold (Lancashire), The Bold in Aston Botterell (Shropshire), Booden Farm in Haughton, Booth in Blithfield, and Bull Bridge in Penkridge (all Staffordshire). Compare Bodle .
English: nickname from Middle English bold, Old English b(e)ald ‘bold, brave, noble’, which in Middle English developed the additional sense ‘arrogant, impudent, shameless’.
English: variant of   Bolt   and possibly a variant of   Bald   2.

variants.name: Bollmann
meanings: German:
elaborated derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo (see Bold 4).

topographic name for a man who lived on a rounded hill, from Middle Low German bol ‘rounded hill’ + man ‘man’, or a habitational name for someone from any of several places called Boll (see Boll ). Compare Bolman .

variants.name: Bowlds
meanings: English: possibly a variant of   Bold  .

variants.name: Boldman
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German Boldemann: from a compound of a variant of the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo (see Bold 4) + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bodle
meanings: English: habitational name from Buddle Oak in Halse (Somerset), Buddle in Fordingbridge (Hampshire), Buddle in Niton (Isle of Wight), Budleigh in Moretonhampstead (Devon), or Budle in Bamburgh (Northumberland), all named from Middle English bothel, Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’, probably denoting a homestead of some considerable size. See also Bold .
English (West Midlands): variant of   Beadle  .

variants.name: Bolds
meanings: English: variant of Bold , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Boldt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hermann, Kurt, Bernhard, Dieter, Egon, Gerd, Gerhard, Gunter, Gunther, Heinz, Hilde.
German and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, a short form of various compound names with the first element bald ‘bold’. See also German Bold , compare Bolt .

variants.name: Bolda
meanings: Polish: from the German personal name Bold (see Bold 4).
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name Boldizsár, Hungarian form of Baltazar .

variants.name: Bolt
meanings: English: from Middle English bolt ‘bolt, bar’, also ‘bundle’ (Old English bolt ‘arrow’). One of the most common senses of this word was ‘the blunt-headed bolt of a crossbow’, so it could have been given to an archer or to a maker of iron bars or bolts. Bolt was also used metaphorically for a short, thickset, or tubby person and for someone who was physically upright as a bolt, i.e. ‘as straight as an arrow’.
English: variant of   Bold   1.

Dutch, North German, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, a short form of various compound names with the first element bald ‘bold’. See also German Bold , compare German and Danish Boldt .

variants.name: Bolton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in northern England named Bolton (Lancashire, Northumberland, Westmorland, and Yorkshire) or from Boulton in Derbyshire and East Lothian in Scotland, from Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’ (see Bold 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Bolding
meanings: German: patronymic from   Bold   as a personal name.
Danish: habitational name from a place so named in Jutland.
English (Middlesex): variant of   Balding   1.

nameMeanings.name: Leigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Leah
meanings: English: variant of   Leigh  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Bold Leigh of Walton was born in 1448, in Walton on the Hill, Surrey, England as the daughter of Robert Legh. She married Henry Leigh of Walton in 1471, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1492, in Walton, Surrey, England, at the age of 44.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Bold Leigh of Walton(은)는 1448년 영국 수리 월턴  테 힐에서 Robert Legh의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1471년 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 Henry Leigh of Walton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1492년 영국 수리 월턴에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.
